{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZqGkQtHB58eVVTcpTZqa8","type":"tool_result","content":"0\t//! Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\"*\"` for all databases).\n26\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\n27\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t    None = 0,\n43\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t    Read = 1,\n45\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t    Write = 2,\n47\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t    Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// | Input | Role |\n57\t    /// |-------|------|\n58\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\n64\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t        match tag {\n67\t            \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t            \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => Role::Write,\n69\t            \"tag:axon-read\" | \"tag:read\" | \"read\" => Role::Read,\n70\t            _ => Role::None,\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\n75\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t        tags.iter()\n77\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\n78\t            .max()\n79\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n80\t    }\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\n84\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n85\t        match self {\n86\t            Role::None => write!(f, \"none\"),\n87\t            Role::Read => write!(f, \"read\"),\n88\t            Role::Write => write!(f, \"write\"),\n89\t            Role::Admin => write!(f, \"admin\"),\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n96\tpub enum Operation {\n97\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\n98\t    Read,\n99\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\n100\t    Write,\n101\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\n102\t    Admin,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl Operation {\n106\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\n107\t    pub fn required_role(self) -> Role {\n108\t        match self {\n109\t            Operation::Read => Role::Read,\n110\t            Operation::Write => Role::Write,\n111\t            Operation::Admin => Role::Admin,\n112\t        }\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\n117\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\n118\t///\n119\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\n120\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\n121\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\n122\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct EntityFilter {\n125\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\n126\t    pub field: String,\n127\t    /// The required value for that field.\n128\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl EntityFilter {\n132\t    /// Convenience constructor.\n133\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into<String>, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n134\t        Self {\n135\t            field: field.into(),\n136\t            value,\n137\t        }\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\n143\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\n144\t    pub fn matches(&self, data: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {\n145\t        data.get(&self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == &self.value)\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Identity context for a request.\n150\t///\n151\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\n152\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\n153\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n154\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\n155\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\n156\t    pub actor: String,\n157\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\n158\t    pub role: Role,\n159\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\n160\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\n161\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\n162\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n163\t    pub entity_filter: Option<EntityFilter>,\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\timpl CallerIdentity {\n167\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\n168\t    pub fn anonymous() -> Self {\n169\t        Self {\n170\t            actor: \"anonymous\".into(),\n171\t            role: Role::Admin,\n172\t            entity_filter: None,\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\n177\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into<String>, role: Role) -> Self {\n178\t        Self {\n179\t            actor: actor.into(),\n180\t            role,\n181\t            entity_filter: None,\n182\t        }\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\n186\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -> Self {\n187\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\n188\t        self\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\n194\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\n195\t    pub fn check(&self, op: Operation) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n196\t        let required = op.required_role();\n197\t        if self.role >= required {\n198\t            Ok(())\n199\t        } else {\n200\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n201\t                \"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\",\n202\t                self.role, op, required\n203\t            )))\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// Admin users see all fields. .ddx/beads.jsonl:347:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go implements NewPostAttemptTriageHook or an equivalently named constructor returning func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error), with explicit injected AgentRunner/service/config inputs and no dependency on cli/cmd.
2. TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary asserts the hook reaches an injected AgentRunner.Run path and a static grep/AST assertion proves triage_hook.go does not import os/exec or shell out to ddx agent run.
3. TestTriageHook_PromptIncludesOutcomeAndLogExcerpt verifies the prompt starts with MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes bead content, ExecuteBeadReport JSON, and a bounded session-log excerpt when available.
4. TestLoop_TriageHook_FiresPostOutcome proves PostAttemptTriageHook runs after report status/detail are populated and before no-progress cooldown is applied.
5. TestTriageHook_RecordsButDoesNotMutateOutcome proves valid triage classification populates OutcomeReason and/or a triage event without changing ExecuteBeadReport.Status.
6. TestTriageHook_HookError_FallsThroughToLegacyCooldown proves hook error leaves OutcomeReason empty and preserves existing cooldown behavior.
7. The triage prompt requests structured JSON fields classification, recommended_action, rationale, suggested_amendments, and suggested_followup_beads.
8. WIRED-IN: git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9d6892f1b33e72b695119fddcbb54575e0d984c3","created_at":"2026-05-04T15:37:15.580462478Z","description":"PROBLEM
  ddx try needs post-attempt triage after the loop has adjudicated an attempt outcome and before cooldown decisions, so transient failures can be classified without mutating the underlying status. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.
  - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.
  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: "bead-lifecycle-triage"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.
  - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.
  - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.
  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.
  - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract exists.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"sonnet","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-04T21:48:52.452461738Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

This bead is a roll-up epic. Each acceptance criterion was delivered by an earlier
child bead; the current worktree (base aff3240b) already satisfies every AC. Prior
execute-bead attempts produced empty diffs because there is no per-attempt code
to write — the children shipped the work. Reviewer kept blocking because the
diff-only inspection does not see across the rollup. Concrete evidence below
maps each AC to its in-tree artifact and (where known) the commit that landed it.

AC 1 — All 7 homepage sections live
  Artifact: website/layouts/index.html
  Sections present (grep '<!-- Section':
    1. .ddx-home-hero        (Hero)
    2. .ddx-home-problem     (Problem)
    3. .ddx-home-how         (How it works — 3 steps)
    4. .ddx-home-features    (Features preview, 4-up grid → /features/)
    5. .ddx-home-principles  (6-card principles grid)
    6. .ddx-home-demo        (Asciinema player)
    7. .ddx-home-cta         (Get started CTA)
  Cross-check: website/DESIGN.md "Homepage Section Map" table lists the same 7
  selectors. Playwright test 'homepage has all 7 sections' asserts each.

AC 2 — /features/ section with maturity badges
  Artifact: website/content/features/_index.md
  Seven feature sections, each ending in a {{< maturity ... >}} shortcode call:
    Artifact Graph (beta), Beads & DAG (stable), Execute-Loop (stable),
    Evidence Capture (stable), Multi-Model Review (beta), Skills (beta),
    Agent-Agnostic Dispatch (stable).
  Shortcode: website/layouts/shortcodes/maturity.html
  Playwright test 'features page loads with maturity badges' asserts visibility
  of .ddx-maturity--stable, .ddx-maturity--beta, .ddx-maturity--planned.

AC 3 — DESIGN.md tokens wired to Hugo
  Artifact: website/DESIGN.md
  Sections: Palette (lever/load/fulcrum/brass), Surface & Foreground,
  Typography (Inter/Space Grotesk/Newsreader), Homepage Section Map,
  Maturity Badges, Responsive Breakpoints.
  Wiring: layouts/index.html:1 imports assets/css/custom.css through
    {{- $css := resources.Get "css/custom.css" | minify | fingerprint -}}
  Tokens (--ddx-accent-lever, --ddx-bg-canvas, etc.) are defined in
  assets/css/custom.css and consumed by .ddx-home-* selectors in layouts/.

AC 4 — Playwright e2e gaps closed
  Artifact: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts (134 lines, 12 tests).
  Coverage:
    - homepage loads with hero and features
    - homepage has all 7 sections (per-section locator assertions)
    - homepage screenshot (full-page, fonts/CDN blocked for stability)
    - homepage fits mobile viewport (390x844, no horizontal scroll)
    - features nav link in homepage
    - features page loads with maturity badges (stable/beta/planned)
    - features page screenshot
    - getting started page
    - CLI reference page
    - skills page
    - plugins page
    - ecosystem page
    - nav links work (Docs / Concepts / Features routing)
  Snapshots committed: e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/{features,getting-started,homepage}-linux.png

AC 5 — UI screenshots committed to website/static/ui/
  Artifacts:
    website/static/ui/homepage.png
    website/static/ui/features.png
  These are the canonical promotional screenshots referenced from marketing
  copy. Playwright snapshot baselines (e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/) are
  the test-time visual regression baselines.

  Residual gap (not blocking AC, noted for follow-up): content/features/_index.md
  references seven additional feature-specific placeholder images
  (feature-artifact-graph.png, feature-beads-dag.png, feature-execute-loop.png,
  feature-evidence-capture.png, feature-multi-model-review.png,
  feature-skills.png, feature-agent-dispatch.png) that do not yet exist in
  static/ui/. The alt text on each marks them "placeholder". Recommend a
  follow-up bead to either (a) generate per-feature screenshots, or (b) drop
  the placeholder image lines until assets are produced.

What is done
  All five acceptance criteria are evidenced by files currently in the
  worktree as listed above. Repository state at base aff3240b is sufficient
  to close this epic.

What is blocking a per-attempt commit
  Nothing in the AC requires new code in this attempt. Producing a fabricated
  diff to "show progress" would be lower quality than a clean no_changes
  exit with this rationale.

What a follow-up attempt would need
  Either (a) close this epic on the strength of in-tree evidence (recommended),
  or (b) split the residual feature-image gap into a fresh bead targeting only
  static/ui/feature-*.png generation and drop this rollup's hold on the queue.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:397:{"acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/<id>/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.

AC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.

AC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.

AC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.

Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):

  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)
  Harness: claude / model: sonnet
  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls
  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit
  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,
    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts
  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)
  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.

The same pattern appeared on:
  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)
  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)
  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)
  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)

Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}

No frontend component renders the ArtifactType definitions for an artifact. When an artifact matches a type definition (by prefix), the operator has no UI surface to view the associated template, prompt, and examples. The detail-view of any artifact is missing the type-aware panel.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/ contains artifact detail pages but no ArtifactTypePanel.svelte component.
- The typeDefinitions resolver (ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep) provides the data, but nothing in the frontend consumes it.
- On prefix collision (multiple type definitions matching the same prefix), no selector dropdown or ?typeDef= URL state exists.
- prefixOf utility at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/artifacts/grouping.ts (from AC #10 of parent) is the expected import source.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte:
  - Tabbed layout: Reference Prompt | Template | Examples tabs (labels neutral, not 'Generate from').
  - On prefix collision: selector dropdown with ?typeDef= URL round-trip.
  - Imports prefixOf from artifacts/grouping.ts (single source of truth).
- Integrate ArtifactTypePanel into the artifact detail-view page.
- Add vitest unit tests for the component.

NON-SCOPE
- The typeDefinitions GraphQL resolver (that's ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep).

.ddx/beads.jsonl:443:{"acceptance":"1. Node(id) at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go resolves Bead IDs (prefix "bead-") via the bead store scoped to context WorkingDir.
2. Node(id) resolves Execution run IDs (prefix "exec-" or whatever the run-id format is) via the executions store scoped to context WorkingDir.
3. Returns nil + appropriate error for IDs that don't match any known prefix.
4. Test: TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject — register two projects with overlapping bead IDs; query Node("bead-X") via project A's scoped GraphQL; assert returns project A's bead, not B's.
5. Test: TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject — same shape for execution runs.
6. Test: TestNode_ReturnsNilForUnknownPrefix — assert no panic, nil returned.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:08.83276105Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-5ae050dc","depends_on_id":"ddx-4c51d33e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:15Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5ae050dc","depends_on_id":"ddx-055e8d32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:15Z"}],"description":"LAYER 3 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. Completeness work — fixes incomplete Relay spec compliance.

PROBLEM
cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T03:18:44.212041723Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"exit status 1

3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.
4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.
7. Tests:
   - TestLoop_DisruptedExecution_NoCooldown — context cancelled mid-execution; assert no execute-loop-retry-after set; bead returns to ready
   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set
   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)
   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events
8. Manual verification: kill an in-flight worker mid-execution; assert the bead is immediately re-claimable by the next worker (no 6h park).

Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Optional exponential backoff was correctly skipped per AC.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:450:{"acceptance":"1. TestReadySortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies an explicit lower `queue-rank` sorts before unranked and higher-ranked beads of the same priority.
2. TestReadyExecutionSortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies ReadyExecution uses the same queue-rank order after execution-ready filtering.
3. TestQueueRankDoesNotCrossPriorityBoundary verifies a ranked P1 bead never sorts before an unranked P0 bead.
4. TestQueueRankMissingSortsAfterExplicitRankWithinPriority verifies missing queue-rank sorts after explicit ranks in the same priority bucket.
5. TestQueueRankNumericStringCompatibility verifies numeric-string queue-rank values are read compatibly.
6. WIRED-IN: sortBeadsForQueue, used by Ready and ReadyExecution, calls the queue-rank helper before created_at/id tie-breakers.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run "TestReady.*QueueRank|TestQueueRank" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-06T15:58:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"963074","created_at":"2026-05-06T13:25:21.924949678Z","description":"PROBLEM
FEAT-004 and SD-004 now require `queue-rank` to order ready beads within a priority bucket, but the store still ignores queue-rank. Operators currently have to change `priority` to force a bead to the top, which changes urgency semantics and can reorder unrelated work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:104-119 defines canonical ready-queue ordering as priority, queue-rank, created_at, id, and says queue-rank must not cross priority or bypass execution filters.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:196-209 defines the same deterministic queue derivation and numeric compatibility rules.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1346-1355 exposes Ready and ReadyExecution, but both delegate to readyFiltered without queue-rank-specific behavior.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1702-1711 sorts only by Priority, CreatedAt, and ID.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small queue-rank parser/helper in cli/internal/bead (acceptable names: QueueRank, queueRank, or parseQueueRank) that accepts integer values and numeric strings from Bead.Extra["queue-rank"].
- Update sortBeadsForQueue to sort by priority asc, explicit queue-rank asc with missing rank after explicit rank inside the same priority, created_at asc, then id asc.
- Keep ReadyExecution filtering unchanged: blocked, cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible=false, and epic-only beads must be excluded before sorting.
- Add focused store tests in cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go or queue-specific test file.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add queue-rank as a Bead struct field or known schema field.
- Do not implement `ddx bead queue` CLI commands in this bead.
- Do not change priority validation, dependency semantics, or claim semantics.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T13:39:27.800718075Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T133547-af606d56","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":2651939,"output_tokens":10389,"total_tokens":2662328,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":218208,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T13:39:28.041338247Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=2662328 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-06T13:39:35.982362509Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

.ddx/beads.jsonl:458:{"acceptance":"1. TD authored for the axon-backend integration (data model mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, multi-machine concurrency story). 2. Axon-backend implementation lands behind a feature flag or selectable backend in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. 3. Migration tool moves an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl into the axon backend without data loss. 4. Existing chaos_test.go suite passes against the axon backend. 5. bd fallback documented (single artifact under docs/) showing how to wire bd as the backend if axon adoption stalls. 6. Performance: queue ops (ready/blocked/show) at parity or better than JSONL on the current 1100-bead archive scale.","closing_commit_sha":"0feba64fbf7574c64276718ec9fe70ddc79e1366","created_at":"2026-05-02T13:06:50.801746517Z","description":"The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-02T14:49:18.951016146Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260502T144455-ed29df8c","harness":"claude","input_tokens":44,"output_tokens":16723,"total_tokens":16767,"cost_usd":1.6210455,"duration_ms":260420,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-02T14:49:18.964923165Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=16767 cost_usd=1.6210"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-02T14:49:25.337055277Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact. artifact: .ddx/executions/20260503T020341-8fe56f98/reviewer-stream.log","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24.065918108Z","kind":"review","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"REQUEST_CHANGES"},{"actor":"","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24.086274671Z","kind":"reopen","source":"","summary":"review: REQUEST_CHANGES"},{"actor":"erik","body":"post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**`cachedTokens` is a dangling schema field.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1178` and `cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go` (Run.CachedTokens) declare the field, but `sessionEntryToRun` in `cli/internal/server/state_runs.go` never assigns it, and `agent.SessionIndexEntry` has no corresponding source field. Either add `CachedTokens` to `SessionIndexEntry` (sourced from harness usage) and project it in `sessionEntryToRun`, or drop the schema field until a backing source exists — shipping a non-nullable-looking attribute that is permanently null is a regression on the lossless-join contract.
**`prompt`/`response`/`stderr` not projected onto run-layer Runs.** The bead body lists these as AgentSession fields not present on Run today. `SessionIndexEntry` doesn't carry them, but the related `Session` struct in `cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:66-70` has `Prompt/Response/Stderr`. `sessionEntryToRun` should hydrate these (likely via the existing session lookup path used elsewhere) so a `run(id:)` query against a run-layer record is truly lossless against AgentSession.
**Test coverage gap matches the projection gap.** `state_runs_test.go` should additionally assert `cachedTokens` (or remove it) and `prompt`/`response`/`stderr` round-trip on the orphan-session case once those projections land.

Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no changes to state_runs.go, no resolver join with AgentSession, no new layering test. Cannot verify any AC item from this diff.

**`cachedTokens` is a dangling schema field.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1178` and `cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go` (Run.CachedTokens) declare the field, but `sessionEntryToRun` in `cli/internal/server/state_runs.go` never assigns it, and `agent.SessionIndexEntry` has no corresponding source field. Either add `CachedTokens` to `SessionIndexEntry` (sourced from harness usage) and project it in `sessionEntryToRun`, or drop the schema field until a backing source exists — shipping a non-nullable-looking attribute that is permanently null is a regression on the lossless-join contract.
**`prompt`/`response`/`stderr` not projected onto run-layer Runs.** The bead body lists these as AgentSession fields not present on Run today. `SessionIndexEntry` doesn't carry them, but the related `Session` struct in `cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:66-70` has `Prompt/Response/Stderr`. `sessionEntryToRun` should hydrate these (likely via the existing session lookup path used elsewhere) so a `run(id:)` query against a run-layer record is truly lossless against AgentSession.
**Test coverage gap matches the projection gap.** `state_runs_test.go` should additionally assert `cachedTokens` (or remove it) and `prompt`/`response`/`stderr` round-trip on the orphan-session case once those projections land.

rationale: All acceptance criteria for ddx-6aedf547 are already satisfied by code
merged into main in commit d9491735 ("feat(graphql): add runRequeue
mutation with idempotent atomic claims"). No new code change is needed
in this attempt.

Evidence by AC:

  1. runRequeue mutation in schema
     - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3115-3116
       defines `runRequeue(input: RunRequeueInput!): RunRequeueResult!`
       under type Mutation.
     - Input/result types declared at schema.graphql:3213-3231.

  2. idempotencyKey REQUIRED
     - schema.graphql:3214-3221 declares `idempotencyKey: String!`
       inside `RunRequeueInput` (non-null), so the GraphQL layer
       rejects requests missing the key.
     - Resolver re-checks at runtime:
       cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_runs.go:30-32
       returns "runRequeue: idempotencyKey is required" if blank.
     - Covered by TestRunRequeue_MissingIdempotencyKey
       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:243).

  3. Duplicate-key returns existing requeued bead, not error
     - resolver_mutation_runs.go:61-73 looks the key up in the
       idempotency cache under a per-resolver mutex. On hit it returns
       `RunRequeueResult{Bead: <existing>, Deduplicated: true}` with
       no second reopen and no second audit event.
     - Covered by TestRunRequeue_DuplicateKeyReturnsExisting
       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:118).

  4. Audit event on the originating bead
     - resolver_mutation_runs.go:82-106 appends a bead.BeadEvent of
       kind `run_requeue` (constant RunRequeueEventKind) to the
       originating bead, with body recording operator identity, run
       id, idempotency key, and the optional layer override.
     - Covered by TestRunRequeue_ReopensBeadAndAuditLogs
       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:58), which asserts the event
       kind, body fields, and that the bead status is reopened.

  5. Tests cover concurrent re-queue with same key
     - TestRunRequeue_ConcurrentSameKey
       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:180) fires N concurrent
       RunRequeue calls with the same idempotencyKey and asserts
       exactly one reopen + one audit event, with all other callers
       receiving Deduplicated=true and the same bead.

The verification command below runs all four RunRequeue tests, which
together exercise every AC item. Exit 0 demonstrates the behavior is
already in place.

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run TestRunRequeue -count=1 -timeout 120s

rationale: Bead ddx-6d132f62 (Story 16.0) is already fully implemented in HEAD by an
earlier attempt that landed via merge commit fd9f2dee → feat commit 0b1b3964
("feat(server): run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file
confinement [ddx-6d132f62]"). The current base 5d5d0960 is a checkpoint
created AFTER that merge; running `git log --oneline -- <story-16 files>`
shows commit 0b1b3964 already in HEAD's ancestry.

Every AC item is satisfied by code already in HEAD:

- Run resolver exposes prompt/response/stderr/toolCalls/bundleFiles/bundleFile
  → cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql lines 1159-1228 (Run
    type extension + RunBundleFile, RunBundleFileContent, RunToolCall,
    RunToolCallConnection types).
  → cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go lines 70-96
    (RunToolCalls and RunBundleFile resolvers; nil-on-miss = 404).

- ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time, not parsed from logs at resolver:
  → cli/internal/agent/types.go ToolCallEntry struct.
  → cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go drainServiceEvents() builds
    []ToolCallEntry from agentlib.ServiceEvent stream.
  → cli/internal/agent/session_index.go SessionIndexEntryFromResult copies
    Result.ToolCalls into SessionIndexEntry.ToolCalls (verified by
    TestSessionIndexEntryFromResult_PersistsToolCalls).
  → cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go GetRunToolCallsGraphQL reads
    SessionIndexEntry.ToolCalls (no log parsing).

- Bundle-root confinement (canonical-path; rejects .., symlink escape,
  absolute paths):
  → cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go resolveRunBundlePath().
  → Tests: TestRunBundleFile_PathTraversalRejected,
    TestRunBundleFile_SymlinkEscapeRejected,
    TestResolveRunBundlePath_DotDotEscape.

- Whitelist + 64KB cap (allowed: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md,
  result.json):
  → state_runs_detail.go runBundleInlineExtensions, runBundleInlineMaxBytes,
    readRunBundleFile() returns Truncated=true with SizeBytes when over cap
    or non-whitelisted.
  → Tests: TestRunBundleFile_SizeCap, TestRunBundleFile_WhitelistEnforcement.

- Resolver unit tests cover all of the above (paths, sizes, whitelist,
  drain-time persistence): see cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail_test.go
  and cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go.

The prior REVIEW:BLOCK on this bead's notes referenced a re-attempt whose
diff contained only a result.json. That review was correct about that
specific re-attempt's diff but did not account for the implementation
already being in main. There is no further code to add for this AC.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:543:{"acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go contains at minimum the 9 test cases enumerated in the description. Shared helper newScriptHarnessRepo lives in cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go. Every test uses the script harness (not virtual, not fakeAgentRunner) and a real temp git repo created via git init with scrubbed GIT_* env. Every test verifies its assertion by running real git commands on the temp repo (git log, git rev-list, git for-each-ref) — no mock git. The suite runs under `go test ./internal/agent -run Integration -race -count=1` in under 60 seconds with all cases passing. Test 5 (FiveConcurrentBeads) proves linear history invariant that ddx-6aa50e57 acceptance demands. Test 6 (TwoWorkersSameBead) proves atomic claim invariant that ddx-3315dce2 acceptance demands. Test 7 (RebaseConflict) depends on the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 being in place — it may be a t.Skip if the coordinator hasn't landed yet, with a TODO comment and a bead reference.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:50:02.413040703Z","description":"Build the integration test suite for execute-loop orchestration using the script harness + real git temp repos. This is the test gap the opus test-plan review identified as the most critical coverage hole: every "concurrency" or "merge race" test today uses in-memory fakes with per-worker orchestrators, so the shared-writer invariant the meta-bead design depends on is never actually exercised.

## Test infrastructure helper

Build a shared helper `newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T) (projectRoot, initialRev string)` in a new file `cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go` that:

1. Creates a temp dir via t.TempDir()
2. Runs `git init` with scrubbed GIT_* env (so the test's own pre-commit hook env doesn't leak)
3. Creates and commits an initial seed commit on `main`
4. Seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with N ready beads (parameterized by test)
5. Returns projectRoot + the initial SHA

The helper must NOT inherit any GIT_DIR from the parent test environment.

## Test cases

Create cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go with these cases. Each runs against the script harness and real git:

1. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged** — one bead, directive appends a line and commits, asserts git log main shows +1 commit, worktree is cleaned up, bead closed with closing_commit_sha.

2. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges** — directive no-op, asserts outcome=no-changes, bead stays open (below adjudication threshold), no commit on main.

3. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized** — directive creates files but omits explicit commit, asserts SynthesizeCommit fires, outcome=merged, single synthetic commit on main with the expected files.

4. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FailedExit_WithCommits_Preserved** — directive commits then set-exit 1, asserts outcome=preserved, refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<sha> exists, main unchanged.

5. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_LinearHistory** — seed 5 distinct beads, spawn 5 workers concurrently (single store, single projectRoot), assert `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0, `git rev-list main ^initial | wc -l` is 5, every bead closed. **This is the ddx-6aa50e57 acceptance test that cannot be written today.**

6. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_TwoWorkersSameBead_ClaimedOnce** — seed 1 bead, spawn 2 workers against the same store, assert exactly one executes and the other reports no_ready_work. **This is the ddx-3315dce2 acceptance test.**

7. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_RebaseConflict_Preserved** — two beads both modify the same line in the same file, workers start from the same base, first lands cleanly, second gets rebase conflict and is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/. Main has exactly +1 commit, ref exists for bead 2. (Only passes once the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 is in place.)

8. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations** — two beads, cancel context after first completes, assert second bead is not claimed. **This is the regression for commit 21d16e6.**

9. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast** — directive writes a no_changes_rationale.txt citing a commit SHA, asserts the bead closes as already_satisfied without the 3-strike cooldown wait.

Each test runs in isolation, cleans up its temp dir, and completes in under 10s. The full suite runs in under 60s. Use `-race` to catch any coordinator goroutine races.

## Out of scope

- Server-layer wiring (that's bead C)
- Real-AI acceptance tests (gated tier 4)
- Multi-project isolation (a separate bead, not filed here)
- Cross-machine scenarios (multi-machine topology bead)","id":"ddx-6f6bf12a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:integration","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"Integration tests for execute-loop orchestration using script harness + real git","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.671814475Z"}

No performance benchmark exists comparing axon backend vs JSONL backend at realistic archive scale (1100 beads). Without this benchmark, the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) cannot evaluate its AC6 gate ('axon parity-or-better than JSONL for ready/blocked/show queue ops').

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/ contains backend_jsonl.go but no axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5, a dep, must land first).
- No benchmark file exercises ready/blocked/show operations on a 1100-bead corpus.
- The missing dep edge (ddx-781a15cf → ddx-95ec5ed5) caused the queue to surface this bead prematurely (dep edge should be added via ).

PROPOSED FIX
- After ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon backend) and ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface) land:
  - Add cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go (or bead_bench_test.go) with:
    - BenchmarkReady_JSONL_1100: ready queue ops on 1100-bead JSONL corpus.
    - BenchmarkReady_Axon_1100: same ops on 1100-bead axon corpus.
    - BenchmarkBlocked_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkBlocked_Axon_1100.
    - BenchmarkShow_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkShow_Axon_1100.
  - Generate 1100-bead test corpus (mix of open + closed + archived).
  - Capture wall-time and allocs. Commit results to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/axon-vs-jsonl-benchmark.md.

NON-SCOPE
- axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5).

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend.

The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely.

Recommended follow-up:
  1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5
     (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands)
  2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first.
  3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends.

Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/.

Evidence checked:
- `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob).
- No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep).
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`.
- TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate.
- Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open:
  * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client"
  * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"
  * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend"
  * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead)

Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

done:
- Added backend-specific benchmarks for Ready, Blocked, and Show against JSONL and Axon.
- Built a 1100-bead corpus with active, closed, and archived records.
- Verified `go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.

blocker:
- The axon backend implementation needs further performance work before the benchmark results can satisfy the bead's AC3 parity gate.

follow_up:
- Optimize the axon ReadAll / archive-read path and rerun `go test -run '^$' -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/bead/...` until the benchmark document can honestly record parity-or-better results.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: parent bead ddx-7b753655 was decomposed into three focused child slices because it exceeds the size-check thresholds (>6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems; mixes backend GraphQL + frontend Svelte migration + Playwright e2e + clock-driven integration test as separate top-level deliverables; >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages — the existing workers panel +layout.svelte alone is 438 LOC).

Children created (parent=ddx-7b753655):

- 5a (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-28f710ee):
  Backend derived-view GraphQL workers query + resolver reading from
  s.workerIngest registry (cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go).
  Adds schema fields: id, project, harness, state (freshness via
  freshnessState()), lastEventAt, mirrorFailuresCount,
  hadDroppedBackfill, currentBead, currentAttempt. Includes
  TestGraphQL_Workers_FreshnessFields and
  TestGraphQL_Workers_MultipleWorkersPerProject. ~150 LOC.

- 5b (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-86d32a20):
  Frontend Svelte workers panel migration at
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/.
  Surfaces freshness indicator, duplicate-worker display, and the
  "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" UI labeling. Includes
  Playwright e2e covering the new fields against fixture state.
  Depends on 5a. ~400 LOC.

- 5c (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-7888e046):
  Wired-in integration test TestWorkersPanel_LiveTransitions —
  starts a real worker, asserts connected→stale (after 2× probe
  interval)→disconnected (after 10× probe interval) using a
  clock-injection seam in worker_ingest.freshnessState(). Not
  fixture-based. Depends on 5a. ~150 LOC + small clock seam.

Dependency edges added: 5b → 5a, 5c → 5a (via `ddx bead dep add`).
Parent ddx-7b753655 updated with --notes recording the decomposition.

Why this is the right cut: 5a is a self-contained backend feature
testable in isolation and unlocks the other two. 5b and 5c each
exercise a different surface (UI vs live-data integration test) and
will land independently once 5a is merged.

rationale: Bead ddx-7e7b12f0 is already satisfied by commit 3b0fefc1 ("test(web): e2e
regression check for doc graph edge color tokens [ddx-7e7b12f0]"), which is an
ancestor of HEAD in this worktree. The required test file is present at
cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts
and contains:

  AC1: file exists at the specified route path (verified by `git ls-files`).
  AC2: asserts >=1 <line> SVG edge via `expect(edges.first()).toBeAttached()`
       and `expect(await edges.count()).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)`.
  AC3: asserts edge stroke and arrow marker fill resolve to rgb(75, 85, 99)
       (#4B5563) in the default light theme; uses an addInitScript pre-load to
       clear any persisted .dark class for determinism.
  AC4: toggles document.documentElement.classList.add('dark') and re-asserts
       both stroke and marker fill resolve to rgb(184, 175, 156) (#B8AF9C).
  AC5: file is wired into the existing playwright project (sibling of
       drain.e2e.ts / personas.e2e.ts) and uses the same in-test page.route
       GraphQL mocking pattern, so `bun run test:e2e` discovers and runs it.

The previous BLOCKING reviews compared only the most recent attempt's diff and
correctly noticed it contained no test file — that is because the file was
already committed in an earlier attempt and there was nothing left to add.
There is no remaining code change to make for this bead.

The verification command below proves the file is present at the expected path
and contains the four token-string assertions that AC2-AC4 require.

verification_command: test -f "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "rgb(75, 85, 99)" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "rgb(184, 175, 156)" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "svg.locator('line')" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "classList.add('dark')" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts"
